Richard M. Lee of SELMAN BREITMAN (415-979-0400), appearing for defendant / joinder Aurora Pump Company

RULING:
DEFENDANT MERLEX STUCCO, INC.'S MOTION TO ABATE ENTIRE ACTION PENDING JOINDER OF ALL NECESSARY PARTIES

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Merlex Stucco, Inc.'s motion to abate entire action pending joinder of all necessary parties is denied. Heirs are necessary but not indispensable parties to a wrongful death action. (Ruttenberg v. Ruttenberg (1997) 53 Cal.App.4th 801.) All necessary parties were initially joined as plaintiffs when this action was filed. Jeff London and Brent Revok later voluntarily dismissed their claims with prejudice. Mr. London and Mr. Revok therefore will not be able to later bring a wrongful death action against defendant. (See, e.g., Boeken v. Philip Morris USA, Inc. (2010) 48 Cal.4th 788; Alpha Mechanical, Heating & Air Conditioning, Inc. v. Travelers Casualty & Surety Co. (2005) 133 Cal.App.4th 1319.) No abatement is required and this court maintains the power to adjudicate this action. All joinders to the Merlex Stucco's motion are denied. One order for motion and joinders signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)
